title: Mark Carney

infobox.value: Mark Joseph Carney  (1965-03-16) March 16, 1965 (age 61)  [Fort Smith, Northwest Territories](/wiki/Fort_Smith,_Northwest_Territories "Fort Smith, Northwest Territories"), Canada

* [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_University "Harvard University") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) * [St Peter's College, Oxford](/wiki/St_Peter%27s_College,_Oxford "St Peter's College, Oxford") ([MPhil](/wiki/Master_of_Philosophy "Master of Philosophy")) * [Nuffield College, Oxford](/wiki/Nuffield_College,_Oxford "Nuffield College, Oxford") ([DPhil](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy"))

July 1, 2013 – March 15, 2020 | | | Appointed by | [George Osborne](/wiki/George_Osborne "George Osborne") | | Preceded by | [Sir Mervyn King](/wiki/Sir_Mervyn_King "Sir Mervyn King") | | Succeeded by | [Andrew Bailey](/wiki/Andrew_Bailey_(banker) "Andrew Bailey (banker)") | | 2nd [Chair of the Financial Stability Board](/wiki/Financial_Stability_Board#Chairs "Financial Stability Board") | | | **In office** November 4, 2011 – November 26, 2018 | | | Preceded by | [Mario Draghi](/wiki/Mario_Draghi "Mario Draghi") | | Succeeded by | [Randal Quarles](/wiki/Randal_Quarles "Randal Quarles") | | 8th [Governor of the Bank of Canada](/wiki/Governor_of_the_Bank_of_Canada "Governor of the Bank of Canada") | | | **In office** February 1, 2008 – June 3, 2013 | | | Prime Minister | [Stephen Harper](/wiki/Stephen_Harper "Stephen Harper") | | Preceded by | [David A. Dodge](/wiki/David_A._Dodge "David A. Dodge") | | Succeeded by | [Stephen Poloz](/wiki/Stephen_Poloz "Stephen Poloz") | | [Deputy Governor of the Bank of Canada](/wiki/Deputy_Governor_of_the_Bank_of_Canada "Deputy Governor of the Bank of Canada") | | | **In office** August 10, 2003 – November 15, 2004 | | | Prime Minister | [Paul Martin](/wiki/Paul_Martin "Paul Martin") | | Governor | [David A. Dodge](/wiki/David_A._Dodge "David A. Dodge") | | Preceded by | [Paul Jenkins](/wiki/Paul_Jenkins_(economist) "Paul Jenkins (economist)") | | Succeeded by | [Tiff Macklem](/wiki/Tiff_Macklem "Tiff Macklem") | | |

(born March 16, 1965) is a Canadian politician and economist who has served as the 24th [prime minister of Canada](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Canada "Prime Minister of Canada") since 2025. He has also been [leader of the Liberal Party](/wiki/Leader_of_the_Liberal_Party_of_Canada "Leader of the Liberal Party of Canada") and the [member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(Canada) "Member of Parliament (Canada)") (MP) for [Nepean](/wiki/Nepean_(federal_electoral_district) "Nepean (federal electoral district)") since 2025. He previously was [Governor of the Bank of Canada](/wiki/Governor_of_the_Bank_of_Canada "Governor of the Bank of Canada") from 2008 to 2013 and [Governor of the Bank of England](/wiki/Governor_of_the_Bank_of_England "Governor of the Bank of England") from 2013 to 2020.
